author: gcolvin
content: I moved part of the Abstract to Motivation, moved the Conclusion to Rationale, put the all of the information under Specification and it still reads OK, if a little strange.

author: gcolvin
content:
All tests pass but it still won't merge, because this comment:
https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/pull/11353#pullrequestreview-3870273142
was marked by <@792822129019584562> as a "requested change" .

Somebody with write access has to merge it.  Please.

author: gcolvin
content:
Yes, <@508125616940515329> had a similar concern, which I answered there.  I think it's fits the requirements for an Informational EIP:
> An Informational EIP describes an Ethereum design issue, or provides general guidelines or information to the Ethereum community, but does not propose a new feature. Informational EIPs do not necessarily represent Ethereum community consensus or a recommendation, so users and implementers are free to ignore Informational EIPs or follow their advice.
  I am reposting my answer to Jochem here so we can discuss this.

author: gcolvin
content: > Proposals to provide for static control flow have repeatedly been rejected at very late stages in our process. EIP-2315 was actually removed from the code of a fork that was ready to ship. One reason for these years of wasted effort is that too many people in the community simply don't understand the very wide range of relevant industry experience and computer science. Computer architecture, graph theory, interpreters, compilers, formal analysis, blockchain security, cryptography, zero knowledge and more are all relevant, and people cannot be expected to be experts in all of them. I'm not. I do not want to see the current proposals fail for the same reasons. Therefore I insist on explaining the issues in a prominent, permanent document that can be carefully reviewed by experts and linked to by the proposals themselves.

author: gcolvin
content: Those of you who have been here as long as I have should understand how important this is to me, and how important I believe it is to the community.

author: jochembrouwer
content: Hi Greg, yes I know how important this to you. I also remember reading this text (maybe in another form) before. Let me get back to your reply to the EIP PR 🙂

author: gcolvin
content: If possible, I'd really like to get this pushed soon so I can schedule a break-out call to discuss it and the related Core EIPs with client teams and researchers.  It can always be withdrawn later.
